report_metadata.cancer_type: Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer (NSCLC) — RET Fusion-Positive Adenocarcinoma

treatments.name: Selpercatinib (Retevmo) — First-Line Monotherapy

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 3 RCT (LIBRETTO-431) demonstrated superiority over chemotherapy +/- pembrolizumab in first-line RET fusion-positive NSCLC with PFS HR 0.46 (p<0.001). Supported by Phase 1/2 LIBRETTO-001 with long-term follow-up.

survival_benefit.rationale: Median PFS 24.8 months vs 11.2 months (HR 0.46). ORR 84% vs 65%. Median DoR 24.2 months vs 11.5 months. LIBRETTO-001 final OS 47.6 months in pretreated patients. More than doubles PFS vs standard chemotherapy.

accessibility.rationale: FDA approved (September 2022 for first-line RET fusion-positive NSCLC). Also EMA approved. Widely available at major cancer centers. Oral administration.

safety_profile.rationale: Generally well tolerated. Grade >=3 TRAEs: hypertension (13%), ALT increase (9%), AST increase (6%). Only 3% treatment discontinuation in LIBRETTO-001. Important for CKD patients: selpercatinib causes pseudo-decrease in kidney function by inhibiting tubular creatinine secretion (MATE1 inhibition) — use cystatin C for true renal monitoring.

biomarker_match.rationale: Directly targets the patient's confirmed RET fusion. Selpercatinib is a highly selective RET kinase inhibitor with IC50 of 0.8 nM. Perfect biomarker match for RET fusion-positive adenocarcinoma.

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Selpercatinib is a highly selective, ATP-competitive small molecule inhibitor of the RET receptor tyrosine kinase. It inhibits wild-type RET and multiple mutant RET isoforms including RET fusions (KIF5B-RET, CCDC6-RET), blocking downstream signaling through RAS/MAPK and PI3K/AKT pathways that drive tumor cell proliferation and survival.
biomarker_requirements: RET fusion-positive (confirmed by FISH, NGS, or RT-PCR)
notable_side_effects: Pseudo-decrease in eGFR via MATE1 inhibition (important for CKD monitoring — use cystatin C)

treatments.name: Selpercatinib — Adjuvant Therapy Post-Surgery (LIBRETTO-432)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 3 RCT (LIBRETTO-432) met primary endpoint with statistically significant improvement in EFS vs placebo in stage IB-IIIA RET fusion-positive NSCLC. 151 patients randomized. OS data trending favorable but immature.

survival_benefit.rationale: Statistically significant and clinically meaningful EFS improvement in resected early-stage RET fusion-positive NSCLC. OS trends favor selpercatinib. Particularly relevant for this Stage III patient if surgical resection is feasible.

accessibility.rationale: Selpercatinib already FDA approved for advanced disease. Adjuvant indication supported by LIBRETTO-432 data; regulatory review anticipated. Available at major cancer centers.

safety_profile.rationale: Safety profile consistent with known selpercatinib toxicity. Well tolerated in adjuvant setting with manageable adverse events. CKD consideration same as first-line use.

biomarker_match.rationale: Directly targets confirmed RET fusion. Stage IB-IIIA is the exact population studied in LIBRETTO-432, matching this patient's Stage III disease.

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Same selective RET kinase inhibition as first-line, used in the adjuvant setting following definitive surgery with or without adjuvant chemotherapy to reduce risk of disease recurrence by eliminating residual RET fusion-driven micrometastatic disease.

key_evidence.journal: Pending full publication (Lilly press release, ASCO 2025)

treatments.availability: FDA Approved (advanced); adjuvant indication under regulatory review

treatments.name: Pralsetinib (Gavreto) — First-Line or Second-Line Monotherapy

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 1/2 ARROW trial demonstrated strong efficacy. FDA accelerated approval based on ARROW data. Phase 3 AcceleRET Lung trial vs SOC results still pending. No Phase 3 superiority data yet unlike selpercatinib.

survival_benefit.rationale: Treatment-naive ORR 77.6% (ARROW). Pretreated ORR 63.1% with median OS 44.3 months. Median DoR 38.8 months in pretreated patients. Intracranial response rate 53% in brain metastases.

accessibility.rationale: FDA approved for advanced RET fusion-positive NSCLC. Oral administration 400mg once daily. Listed in NCCN guidelines. Available in US and many countries.

safety_profile.rationale: Grade >=3 TRAEs: neutropenia (20%), anemia (12%), hypertension (12%). 10% treatment discontinuation rate — higher than selpercatinib. Neutropenia requires monitoring. One death from treatment-related pneumonia reported.

biomarker_match.rationale: Selective RET inhibitor directly targeting the patient's RET fusion. Effective across various RET fusion partners (KIF5B-RET, CCDC6-RET, etc.).

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Pralsetinib is a highly selective small molecule RET kinase inhibitor that potently inhibits RET fusions, mutations, and predicted resistance mutations. It blocks RET-mediated cell signaling, inhibiting tumor cell proliferation and promoting apoptosis in RET-dependent cancers.

key_evidence.journal: Annals of Oncology / The Lancet Oncology

biomarker_requirements: RET fusion-positive (confirmed by FISH, NGS, or RT-PCR)

treatments.name: Concurrent Chemoradiation (Carboplatin/Pemetrexed + RT) + Consolidation Durvalumab

evidence_level.rationale: PACIFIC trial established durvalumab consolidation after CRT as standard of care for unresectable Stage III NSCLC (Phase 3). Well-validated across large patient populations. However, immunotherapy benefit may be limited in RET fusion-positive tumors.

survival_benefit.rationale: Median PFS ~12 months with CRT in stage III NSCLC. Durvalumab consolidation extends PFS. However, RET fusion-positive tumors show limited benefit from checkpoint inhibitors (LIBRETTO-431 subanalysis). 2-year OS ~45-58% with carboplatin-pemetrexed CRT.

accessibility.rationale: Universally available. Carboplatin, pemetrexed, and durvalumab are all FDA approved. Standard treatment at all oncology centers. Radiation therapy widely available.

safety_profile.rationale: Cumulative toxicity from chemoradiation: esophagitis, pneumonitis, myelosuppression. Durvalumab adds immune-mediated adverse events. Carboplatin requires dose adjustment for CKD stage 2 (Calvert formula using GFR). Risk of radiation pneumonitis.

biomarker_match.rationale: Not biomarker-directed. RET fusion-positive tumors show limited sensitivity to checkpoint inhibitors. Chemotherapy provides non-selective cytotoxic activity. IO benefit questionable for this molecular subtype.

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Platinum-based chemotherapy (carboplatin) induces DNA crosslinks and pemetrexed inhibits folate-dependent enzymes. Concurrent radiation causes double-strand DNA breaks. Durvalumab (anti-PD-L1) blocks the PD-1/PD-L1 immune checkpoint pathway to sustain anti-tumor T-cell activity during consolidation.

key_evidence.journal: New England Journal of Medicine / Lung Cancer

biomarker_requirements: None (universal); PD-L1 status may guide durvalumab benefit
notable_side_effects: Nephrotoxicity (carboplatin — requires dose adjustment for CKD stage 2)

treatments.name: Platinum-Based Chemotherapy (Carboplatin + Pemetrexed)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 3 level evidence across multiple large RCTs as backbone of NSCLC therapy. LIBRETTO-431 used this as the control arm. Well-established efficacy and safety data over decades.

survival_benefit.rationale: Median PFS 11.2 months (LIBRETTO-431 control arm for RET+ NSCLC). ORR 65%. Significantly inferior to selpercatinib in RET fusion-positive NSCLC. Median OS ~18.7 months with carboplatin-pemetrexed in Stage III.

accessibility.rationale: Universally available globally. FDA approved. Generic formulations available. Standard at all cancer centers. IV administration requires infusion center access.

safety_profile.rationale: Well-characterized but significant toxicity: myelosuppression, nausea, nephrotoxicity. Carboplatin dose must be adjusted for CKD Stage 2 using Calvert formula with reduced GFR. Pemetrexed generally well-tolerated.

biomarker_match.rationale: Non-targeted cytotoxic therapy. Not biomarker-directed. Inferior to targeted RET inhibition in this molecular subtype. Used as control arm in LIBRETTO-431.

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Carboplatin forms platinum-DNA adducts causing interstrand and intrastrand crosslinks, inhibiting DNA replication and transcription. Pemetrexed is a multi-targeted antifolate that inhibits thymidylate synthase (TS), dihydrofolate reductase (DHFR), and glycinamide ribonucleotide formyltransferase (GARFT), blocking de novo nucleotide synthesis.
notable_side_effects: Nephrotoxicity (carboplatin — dose adjust for CKD Stage 2)

treatments.name: Pembrolizumab + Chemotherapy (Chemo-Immunotherapy)

evidence_level.rationale: KEYNOTE-189 Phase 3 RCT established pembrolizumab + carboplatin/pemetrexed as standard first-line for non-squamous NSCLC. However, LIBRETTO-431 showed this combination is inferior to selpercatinib in RET fusion-positive NSCLC.

survival_benefit.rationale: Used as part of the control arm in LIBRETTO-431 (PFS 11.2 months). RET fusion-positive tumors show limited benefit from checkpoint inhibitors. Retrospective analyses suggest poor immunotherapy response in this molecular subtype.

accessibility.rationale: FDA approved first-line combination for non-squamous NSCLC. Pembrolizumab, carboplatin, and pemetrexed all widely available.

safety_profile.rationale: Immune-mediated adverse events (pneumonitis, colitis, hepatitis, thyroiditis). Chemotherapy toxicity (myelosuppression, nausea). Carboplatin requires dose adjustment for CKD stage 2.

biomarker_match.rationale: Not targeted to RET fusions. RET fusion-positive tumors characteristically have low TMB and limited immunotherapy benefit. PD-L1 expression may be present but does not predict response well in this subtype.

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Pembrolizumab is an anti-PD-1 monoclonal antibody that blocks the PD-1/PD-L1 checkpoint, reactivating anti-tumor T-cell immunity. Combined with carboplatin/pemetrexed cytotoxic chemotherapy. However, RET fusion-positive NSCLCs are typically associated with low tumor mutational burden and limited neoantigen generation, reducing immunotherapy efficacy.

treatments.name: Neoadjuvant Selpercatinib Followed by Surgery

evidence_level.rationale: Case reports and small series demonstrate feasibility of neoadjuvant selpercatinib in stage IIIA RET fusion-positive NSCLC with significant tumor downstaging. No Phase 3 data. Supported by analogy with neoadjuvant targeted therapy in other oncogene-driven NSCLC.

survival_benefit.rationale: Case reports show significant tumor downstaging enabling R0 resection in previously unresectable disease. 84% ORR in treatment-naive patients supports high response probability. Potential for cure with complete resection after downsizing.

accessibility.rationale: Selpercatinib is FDA approved (off-label use in neoadjuvant setting). Requires multidisciplinary tumor board assessment and thoracic surgical expertise. Available at major cancer centers.

safety_profile.rationale: Selpercatinib's known safety profile applies. Perioperative risks must be considered. Drug hold period before surgery required. Same CKD monitoring considerations.

biomarker_match.rationale: Directly targets confirmed RET fusion. Case reports specifically describe Stage IIIA RET fusion-positive adenocarcinoma achieving complete pathological downstaging.

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Pre-surgical administration of selpercatinib to shrink RET fusion-driven tumors and involved lymph nodes, potentially converting unresectable Stage III disease to resectable. High ORR (84%) enables significant tumor volume reduction prior to definitive surgery.

key_evidence.journal: Respirology Case Reports / Frontiers in Oncology

notable_side_effects: Wound healing concerns (drug hold period required)
treatments.availability: Off-label use of FDA-approved drug; clinical trial (NAUTIKA1) ongoing

treatments.name: EP0031/A400 — Next-Generation Selective RET Inhibitor

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 1/2 clinical trial ongoing (NCT05443126). Preclinical data shows activity against G810 solvent front mutations. No mature clinical efficacy data published yet.

survival_benefit.rationale: Preclinical activity against RET solvent front mutations (G810R/S/C) and gatekeeper mutations (V804) that cause resistance to selpercatinib/pralsetinib. Higher brain/plasma ratio than first-gen SRIs. Potential to extend survival beyond first-gen RET inhibitors.

accessibility.rationale: Available only through clinical trial (NCT05443126). Phase 1/2 enrollment at selected sites in US, Europe, and China. Not yet approved for clinical use.

safety_profile.rationale: Phase 1 dose escalation ongoing. Safety profile not yet fully characterized. Expected to have similar class effects to selpercatinib/pralsetinib but with potentially improved selectivity.

biomarker_match.rationale: Designed specifically for RET-altered tumors including RET fusions. Greater potency against common RET fusions and acquired resistance mutations compared to first-generation SRIs.

treatments.mechanism_of_action: EP0031 is a next-generation selective RET inhibitor with enhanced potency against wild-type RET fusions, RET solvent front mutations (G810R/S/C), and gatekeeper mutations (V804). It demonstrates higher selectivity for RET kinase vs VEGFR2 and improved brain penetration compared to selpercatinib and pralsetinib.
biomarker_requirements: RET fusion-positive or RET-mutant solid tumors
notable_side_effects: Expected class effects: hypertension, hepatotoxicity, creatinine elevation

treatments.name: LOXO-260 — Next-Generation RET Inhibitor for Resistant Disease

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 1 first-in-human study ongoing (NCT05241834). Expanded access available (NCT05225259). Preclinical activity against solvent front and gatekeeper mutations. No clinical efficacy data published.

survival_benefit.rationale: Designed to overcome resistance to first-generation RET inhibitors. Preclinical activity against G810 solvent front and V804 gatekeeper mutations alone or in combination. Important sequencing option after selpercatinib/pralsetinib resistance.

accessibility.rationale: Phase 1 clinical trial at select academic centers. Expanded access program available for refractory patients. Developed by Lilly/Loxo Oncology.

safety_profile.rationale: Phase 1 dose escalation in progress. Safety profile not yet established. Expected selective RET inhibitor class effects.

biomarker_match.rationale: Specifically designed for RET-altered tumors refractory to selective RET inhibitors. Maintains potency and selectivity against resistance mutations.

treatments.mechanism_of_action: LOXO-260 is a next-generation selective small-molecule RET kinase inhibitor designed to maintain activity against RET solvent front mutations (G810R/S/C) and gatekeeper mutations (V804L/M), both individually and when co-occurring, which are the primary on-target resistance mechanisms to first-generation RET inhibitors.

key_evidence.journal: Journal of Clinical Oncology (trial in progress)

notable_side_effects: Anticipated selective RET inhibitor class toxicities

treatments.name: Selpercatinib + Crizotinib (for MET-Amplified Resistance)

evidence_level.rationale: Based on 4 single-patient compassionate use protocols. MET amplification identified in ~15% of resistant cases. Partial responses and extended disease control documented but very limited patient numbers.

survival_benefit.rationale: Extended responses demonstrated in 4 patients with MET-amplified resistance after selpercatinib monotherapy. Median PFS with MET amplification alone is only 6.3 months. Combination may overcome bypass resistance.

accessibility.rationale: Both drugs are individually FDA approved. Combination use is off-label and requires compassionate use or clinical trial access. Requires confirmation of MET amplification at resistance.

safety_profile.rationale: Combined toxicities of two kinase inhibitors. Crizotinib adds hepatotoxicity, visual disturbances, GI toxicity. Overlapping hepatotoxicity risk with selpercatinib. CKD monitoring important.

biomarker_match.rationale: Targets both the primary RET fusion driver and the secondary MET amplification resistance mechanism. Requires liquid biopsy or tissue confirmation of MET amplification at progression.

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Dual pathway blockade: selpercatinib continues to inhibit RET kinase signaling while crizotinib blocks MET/ALK/ROS1 bypass signaling. MET amplification is the most common off-target resistance mechanism to selective RET inhibitors, occurring in ~15% of resistant cases.

treatments.name: Cabozantinib (Multikinase Inhibitor with RET Activity)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 2 single-arm trial in RET-rearranged lung cancers. ORR 28%, median PFS 6 months, median OS 10 months. Largely superseded by selective RET inhibitors.

survival_benefit.rationale: Median PFS only 6 months, median OS only 10 months. ORR 28%. Far inferior to selpercatinib (PFS 24.8 months) and pralsetinib (PFS 16.4 months). Only relevant when selective RET inhibitors are unavailable.

accessibility.rationale: FDA approved for other indications (RCC, HCC, MTC). Off-label use for RET fusion-positive NSCLC. Widely available but not specifically indicated.

safety_profile.rationale: Significant toxicity due to multi-kinase inhibition: diarrhea, fatigue, hand-foot syndrome, hypertension, hepatotoxicity. Frequent dose reductions. VEGFR inhibition causes additional off-target effects. Must monitor closely in CKD.

biomarker_match.rationale: Has RET inhibitory activity but is a broad multikinase inhibitor (VEGFR2, MET, AXL, FLT3). IC50 for RET much higher than selective inhibitors. Not optimally designed for RET fusions.

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Cabozantinib is a multi-targeted tyrosine kinase inhibitor that inhibits RET, VEGFR2, MET, AXL, FLT3, and other kinases. Its RET inhibitory activity is less potent and selective compared to selpercatinib and pralsetinib, and its broad kinase inhibition profile contributes to higher toxicity.
treatments.availability: FDA Approved (other indications); off-label for RET+ NSCLC

treatments.name: BOS172738 — Selective RET Inhibitor

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 1 dose-escalation/expansion study completed. Final data (Nov 2023 cutoff) published in ESMO Open 2025. ORR 28% in RET fusion-positive NSCLC and 59% disease control rate.

survival_benefit.rationale: ORR 28% in RET fusion-positive NSCLC with median DoR 10.17 months. DCR 59%. Efficacy lower than selpercatinib/pralsetinib. Primarily relevant for patients who have failed first-line RET inhibitors.

accessibility.rationale: Phase 1 study completed. Not yet advanced to Phase 2/3. Availability limited to clinical trial settings. Development status unclear.

safety_profile.rationale: Grade >=3 TRAEs in 54%. Most common: CPK increase (25%), neutropenia (10%), anemia (9%). Higher grade 3+ toxicity than selpercatinib.

biomarker_match.rationale: Selective RET inhibitor targeting RET fusions and mutations. Activity against some resistance mutations demonstrated preclinically.

treatments.mechanism_of_action: BOS172738 is a potent and selective RET kinase inhibitor that blocks RET-driven signaling in tumors harboring RET fusions or activating RET mutations. It demonstrates activity against some acquired resistance mutations seen after first-generation RET inhibitor therapy.
biomarker_requirements: RET fusion-positive or RET-mutant solid tumors

treatments.name: Vandetanib (Multikinase Inhibitor with RET Activity)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 2 LURET trial: ORR 53% in selected population. GLORY registry real-world data: ORR 18%, median PFS 2.9 months. Limited and inconsistent evidence.

survival_benefit.rationale: Real-world ORR only 18% with median PFS 2.9 months (GLORY registry). Even in LURET trial, PFS was only 4.7 months. Vastly inferior to selective RET inhibitors.

accessibility.rationale: FDA approved for unresectable medullary thyroid cancer. Off-label use for RET fusion-positive NSCLC. Available but carries black box warning.

safety_profile.rationale: BLACK BOX WARNING for QT prolongation, torsades de pointes, and death. Significant GI toxicity, hypertension, liver function test elevations. REMS program required. Very poor safety-benefit ratio for NSCLC use.

biomarker_match.rationale: IC50 for RET is 76 nM vs 0.8 nM for selpercatinib — nearly 100-fold less potent. Multi-kinase inhibitor with predominant VEGFR/EGFR activity. Suboptimal RET selectivity.

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Vandetanib inhibits RET, VEGFR, and EGFR tyrosine kinases. Its anti-RET activity is substantially less potent than selective RET inhibitors (IC50 76 nM vs 0.8 nM for selpercatinib). The broad kinase inhibition contributes to significant off-target toxicity including cardiac effects.
notable_side_effects: QT prolongation and torsades de pointes (BLACK BOX WARNING)
treatments.availability: FDA Approved (MTC); off-label for RET+ NSCLC (REMS required)

clinical_trials.title: Phase 3: Selpercatinib vs Chemotherapy +/- Pembrolizumab in First-Line RET Fusion-Positive NSCLC
clinical_trials.early_results: Met primary endpoint: mPFS 24.8 vs 11.2 months (HR 0.46, p<0.001). ORR 84% vs 65%. Established selpercatinib as first-line standard of care.

clinical_trials.title: Phase 3: Adjuvant Selpercatinib vs Placebo in Stage IB-IIIA RET Fusion-Positive NSCLC After Surgery
clinical_trials.early_results: Met primary endpoint: statistically significant improvement in EFS vs placebo. OS trending in favor of selpercatinib but immature. 151 patients randomized.

clinical_trials.title: Phase 3: First-Line Pralsetinib vs SOC in Advanced RET Fusion-Positive NSCLC
clinical_trials.early_results: ~226 patients randomized 1:1 to pralsetinib 400mg QD vs SOC. Results pending. Supported by ARROW Phase 1/2 data showing ORR 77.6% in treatment-naive patients.

clinical_trials.title: Phase 1/2: EP0031/A400 Next-Generation RET Inhibitor in RET-Altered Solid Tumors
clinical_trials.early_results: Preclinical activity against G810 solvent front mutations and V804 gatekeeper mutations. Higher brain penetration than first-gen RET inhibitors. Dose escalation and expansion ongoing.

clinical_trials.title: Phase 1: LOXO-260 Next-Generation RET Inhibitor in RET Inhibitor-Refractory RET-Altered Cancers
sites: US (UCSF, Dana-Farber, and other academic centers)
biomarker_requirements: Refractory to prior selective RET inhibitor
clinical_trials.early_results: No clinical data published yet. Preclinical activity against solvent front (G810) and gatekeeper (V804) mutations. Expanded access available (NCT05225259).

clinical_trials.title: Phase 2: Neoadjuvant Pralsetinib in Resectable Stage IB-III RET Fusion-Positive NSCLC
clinical_trials.early_results: Evaluating pralsetinib as neoadjuvant therapy. Concept supported by case reports of successful neoadjuvant selpercatinib with tumor downstaging.

clinical_trials.title: Phase 1/2: TPX-0046 (RET/SRC Inhibitor) in Advanced RET-Altered Solid Tumors
clinical_trials.early_results: Preclinical activity against RET G810 solvent front mutations. Dual RET/SRC inhibition may overcome resistance mechanisms.

combination_strategies.rationale: MET amplification is the most common off-target resistance mechanism to selective RET inhibitors (~15% of resistant cases). Four compassionate use cases showed extended responses with dual RET/MET blockade including partial responses and symptom relief after selpercatinib monotherapy failure.

For unresectable Stage III RET fusion-positive NSCLC, integrating selpercatinib with definitive chemoradiation may provide both local control (radiation) and systemic RET-targeted activity. Optimal sequencing (concurrent vs sequential) not yet established in clinical trials.

For patients with EGFR-mutant NSCLC who develop acquired RET fusion as resistance mechanism to osimertinib. ORR 50% (5 evaluable patients), median DoR 11 months. Addresses a specific resistance biology though not directly applicable to primary RET fusion patients.

Perioperative strategy: neoadjuvant selpercatinib for tumor downstaging to enable R0 resection, followed by adjuvant selpercatinib to eliminate micrometastatic disease. Supported by LIBRETTO-432 EFS data and neoadjuvant case reports showing complete pathological responses.

RET signaling intersects with VEGF pathways. Adding anti-angiogenic therapy may enhance RET inhibitor efficacy and address angiogenic bypass resistance. Requires clinical validation in RET-driven NSCLC.

supportive_care.evidence: Temel et al. (NEJM 2010) Phase 3 RCT in metastatic NSCLC; PACO trial (2024); multiple meta-analyses
supportive_care.benefit: Median OS improvement of 2.7 months (11.6 vs 8.9 months) in landmark trial. Improved quality of life, reduced depression, less aggressive end-of-life care. Cost-effective even in resource-limited settings.

supportive_care.evidence: NCCN Survivorship Guidelines v2.2025; multiple systematic reviews and meta-analyses
supportive_care.benefit: Improved cardiopulmonary fitness, reduced cancer-related fatigue, enhanced quality of life. May improve treatment tolerance and reduce treatment-related toxicity. 150 minutes/week moderate aerobic exercise + resistance training recommended.

supportive_care.approach: Renal Function Monitoring and Nephrology Co-Management
supportive_care.evidence: NEJM 2024 (selpercatinib pseudo-decreased kidney function); FDA prescribing information
supportive_care.benefit: Critical for this patient with CKD Stage 2. Selpercatinib inhibits MATE1 causing pseudo-elevated creatinine (18% increase). Use cystatin C-based eGFR for accurate renal monitoring. Carboplatin dose adjustment via Calvert formula using measured GFR. Early nephrology consultation to differentiate true renal decline from drug-related artifact.

supportive_care.evidence: ESPEN guidelines for cancer patients; NCCN Survivorship Guidelines 2025
supportive_care.benefit: NSCLC patients are often malnourished. Nutritional optimization improves treatment tolerance, reduces chemotherapy toxicity, and may improve survival outcomes. Protein intake 1.2-1.5 g/kg/day recommended. Important to maintain adequate caloric intake during targeted therapy.

supportive_care.evidence: NCCN Distress Management Guidelines 2025; multiple RCTs
supportive_care.benefit: Reduces anxiety and depression common in young adults (45 y.o.) with cancer diagnosis. Improved treatment adherence. May include cognitive behavioral therapy, mindfulness-based stress reduction, and support groups. Screen with NCCN Distress Thermometer at each visit.

supportive_care.approach: Bone Health and Calcium/Vitamin D Supplementation
supportive_care.evidence: ASCO/NCCN bone health guidelines; relevant for CKD patients
supportive_care.benefit: CKD Stage 2 patients may have impaired vitamin D metabolism. Cancer treatment can accelerate bone loss. Monitor 25-OH vitamin D levels, PTH, calcium, and phosphorus. Supplement as needed with consideration for renal dosing.
